title.rendered: Amy Ray: Racing in the Street

content.rendered:
How much of a Springsteen fan is Amy Ray?

Enough that for years, she sang “Racing in the Street” in the studio to warm up but rarely dared to cover it in public out of reverence for the original.

Enough that she actually learned the song from the bootleg of Bruce’s Atlanta ’78 show and still has to catch herself from singing “cover all the southern states” because that’s the way Bruce sang it that  night. (There’s actually a clip on YouTube of her singing it that way in 2009.)

And enough that she proves that there’s an exception to my cover rule: sometimes you don’t have to do it better or do it differently–you just have to love the song.

And watching this Amy Ray Band performance from last spring… there’s no question that Amy loves this song.

In a radio interview last year, Amy zeroed in on a particular line of “Racing in the Street” and placed Bruce’s song in a new context:

But regardless of whether Amy’s performance above was channeling the personal or the political, the end result is the same: a gorgeous, heartfelt cover of one of Bruce’s finest songs.

© January 7, 2020

excerpt.rendered: Long-time Springsteen fan Amy Ray loves “Racing in the Street” so much that she almost never covers it (out of reverence for the original). But she covered it last year, and you can watch her gorgeous performance inside.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me, Amy Shark: Secret Garden
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: A surprise cover to close her livestream set, "Secret Garden" proves a perfect match for Amy Shark's soaring vocals and tender arrangement.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Roger Taylor: Racing in the Street
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Queen's Roger Taylor picks up the pace in his cover of Bruce's "Racing in the Street." It moves faster, but is it still moving?

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me, Amy Helm: Atlantic City
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: The Band may have recorded the definitive cover of "Atlantic City," but Amy Helm does her dad proud with her own version.

jetpack-related-posts.title: ROTD/MatR/Cover Me, Gary U.S. Bonds, Bruce Springsteen & The E Street Band, This Little Girl
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Bruce penned "This Little Girl" for Gary U.S. Bonds' comeback album, and it worked--providing Bonds with a Top 40 (and almost a Top 10) hit, powered by the E Street Band and Bruce's lyrics, music, and backing vocals.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Racing in the Street
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: It's one of Bruce's best songs, and it arose from a simple question: And then what?

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me: The Sun Ain’t Gonna Shine Anymore
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: This Walker Brothers wall-of-sound classic features one of Springsteen's finest vocals on record.
